content_of_post_to_comment:
Social media often shows the highlight reel, not the whole story.

Behind every achievement are failures, struggles, and ordinary days that rarely get posted.

Have you ever caught yourself comparing your life to someone's online profile?

What helped you break that habit?
comment: I started noticing that I was comparing my everyday life to other people's carefully selected moments. The thing that helped was following fewer accounts that made me feel behind and paying more attention to the boring progress in my own life. It’s easy to forget that nobody posts the weeks where nothing works out.
